title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: A local decorator is painting the exterior of our house.
questions.explanation: Tests causative 'have something done' in the present continuous.

questions.firstSentence: ‘You should avoid eating too much sugar,’ the nutritionist said.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with 'to-infinitive' or 'against + gerund'.

questions.firstSentence: I regret not visiting my grandmother more often when I was younger.
questions.afterGap: my grandmother more often when I was younger.
questions.explanation: Tests 'wish' + past perfect for past regrets.

questions.firstSentence: It was possible for the document to be lost in the post.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect passive (could have been + past participle).

questions.firstSentence: She never once offered to help with the housework.
questions.explanation: Tests negative inversion (Not once + auxiliary + subject + verb).

questions.firstSentence: The museum was much more interesting than I had anticipated.
